name: Snow Report 2026-03-29 05:50:45
report:
Good morning, Beast family!

A beautiful sunny day is exactly what we wanted yesterday and there’s no shortage of reasons to be out there enjoying it. Our K-1 DJ scene was bumping the stuff you like to dance to, and the vibes are flowing through the weekend. 

Mostly sunny skies return today, and temperatures will top out in the mid-30s at the base and around 29 at the peak with a steady southwest breeze keeping things feeling slightly crisp on the hill. 

Our grooming team was hard at work overnight resurfacing nearly all our open terrain. They swapped Downdraft for Double Dipper while getting to other steeps in Outer Limits and Ovation. Cruiser friendly groomers are also in play like Bittersweet, Rime, Full House and of course, Cruise Control. We can expect more machine-groomed hardpack and frozen granular, yet things were softening up under the sun especially at lower elevations. Looking at next week with a warmup coming, we could see some natural trails open up. For now, head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of options. 

We're scheduled to have 17 lifts up and turning. Key lifts are scheduled to open at 8:00 a.m. with the Canyon and South Ridge Quads joining at 9:00 a.m.

On the kparks front, our Bear parks (Dream Maker & The Stash) are firing once again with a full lineup of features. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again recently. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. 

This weekend will be your last chance to take advantage of our Meet the Mountains Tours, taking place daily at 10:00 and 11:00 a.m., weather permitting. Meet your friendly tour guide in the courtyard at Snowshed Base Lodge and hit the slopes for a 75–90-minute tour of Killington Resort. 

Close out a bangin’ weekend on the slopes! 

- Evan

 

Season passes for 26/27 are now on sale! Lock in the lowest price on Beast 365 or Winter Unlimited passes with lots of perks, plus free skiing & riding for the rest of this spring. Even better news for families: Youth & Child pass pricing has been significantly reduced for next season, with over $300 in savings on Beast 365 access.

Operating Day: 138

Lifts            

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through March 28.           

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.            

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!            

The Tubing Park presented by bubly is closed for the season.        

Uphill Travel            

Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific uphill route information, including a map of each route:  

  • Ramshead base to top of Ramshead Express Quad - Open
  • Ramshead Express Quad to top of Snowdon Mountain - Open
  • Snowshed base to the top of Snowshed Slope - Open
  • Base of Sunrise Village Triple to top of Bear Mountain - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect - CLOSED
  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Pico Summit Glade Route - CLOSED  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                           

MOUNTAIN SERVICES          

Parking & Shuttles          

Parking is available at the K-1, Snowshed, Ramshead, Bear, Skyeship and Vale parking lots. Please respect signage and allow our parking staff to assist you on your trip to the slopes. Shuttle service is available from 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. midweek and 6:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends and holidays.          

Winter car camping and overnight parking is only available in the lot across from Skyeship Lodge. See details here.

All lodges are open daily from 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekdays and 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends/peak dates*.          

K-1 Lodge          

  • Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates.

Snowshed Lodge

  • Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on Fridays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates.

  • Youth Lessons: Youth Group Lessons and our Ministars program meet at Ramshead.
  • Child Care Center: Open weekdays from 8:30 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. and weekends from 8:00 a.m. - 3:45 p.m.          

Bear Mountain Lodge          

  • Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates.
  • Lunch: Open daily with grab & go items and soups available. Full grill menu available Friday - Sunday.
  • Bear Bar: Open from 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m. on weekdays and until 5:00 p.m. on weekends.
  • Guest Services: Available Friday - Monday at ticket windows outside the lodge. Open 30 minutes before lifts open. Kiosk lift ticket pickup is available daily.          

Skyeship Lodge   

  • Food & Bar Service: Available daily from The Rollin’ Rooster.

On-Mountain Dining Options          

Peak Lodge – Open daily from 9:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. Grill open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. Peak Lodge Bar offers its own menu daily with QR code ordering.          

Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill – Ski-on, ski- off. Open Thursday-Monday from 11:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. weather permitting.            

Ledgewood Yurt – closed for the season.  

Mountain services are subject to change. Please check here daily before making your lunch and lodge choices.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-28 05:50:55
report:
Morning, Beast family!

The weekend is here! We’re entering the height of our spring events season, and it all kicks off today during Love Your Peaks, a breast cancer prevention and fundraising event from Boarding for Breast Cancer (B4BC). To join in the fun, register online for the Pink Poker Run, don your best pink outfit, or visit the B4BC booth at our K-1 Lodge and join us in a tribute ride from the top of K-1 at 2:45 p.m. Plus, DJ Benny Shreds will be playing a live set on the K-1 Patio from 2:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. for a lively afternoon of après. At 4:00 p.m., we’re also hosting a special film screening of Queen of the Catskills in our Snowshed Lodge. The fun is only getting started!

Shout out to our grooming team for resurfacing our slopes again. A good chunk of our trails got groomed twice yesterday, making for a much smoother surface than what things could have been following the Thursday night freeze. Without them, we wouldn’t have nearly as much terrain open, so hats off to the crew for doing a great job out there!

Last night, our crew covered around 80 runs across the mountain in fresh corduroy. If you are in for mellow cruisers, check out Easy Street, Snowshed Slope, and Pipe Dream. Panic Button, Downdraft, and Outer Limits will also be getting a groom for those looking to take on steep terrain for a fun challenge. Conditions on groomed terrain will consist of machine-groomed hardpack and frozen granular, and overall, things are riding much better than expected given the weather roller coaster we’ve been on over the past couple of weeks. 

When it gets warmer and softens up early next week, there's a good chance we'll be able to open more natural terrain back up. For now, head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of options. 

Today is calling for clear skies with pockets of sunshine in the mix. Temps will be right around 10 degrees by the time we open, and it's expected to warm up into the teens by the afternoon. With temps as cold as they are, you already know we're firing the snowguns back on. Snowmaking is coming back online on Superstar and Mouse Trap this morning and we should see some great production throughout the day. It’s already spring, but we’re still keeping winter alive as long as we can!  

We're scheduled to have 17 lifts up and turning. It's the final weekend for Skyeship Gondola Stage I, so the next couple of days are your last chances for peak to creek laps. The Bear Mountain Quad is opening for early laps one final time at 7:30 a.m. Park at Bear and score a free coffee hot chocolate in the Bear Mountain Lodge from 7:00 a.m. - 9:00 a.m. Other key lifts are scheduled to open at 8:00 a.m.

In kparks, the parks at Bear (Dream Maker & The Stash) are expected to reopen today with a full lineup of features. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. 

This weekend will be your last chance to take advantage of our Meet the Mountains Tours, taking place daily at 10:00 and 11:00 a.m., weather permitting. Meet your friendly tour guide in the courtyard at Snowshed Base Lodge and hit the slopes for a 75–90-minute tour of Killington Resort. 

There's so much to enjoy even this late in the season, and the party is far from over. Have fun out there! 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-27 16:19:00
report:
Hey, Beast family, 

The weekend is here! We’re entering the height of our spring events season, and it all kicks off tomorrow, March 28th during Love Your Peaks, a breast cancer prevention and fundraising event from Boarding for Breast Cancer (B4BC). To join in the fun, register online for the Pink Poker Run, don your best pink outfit, or visit the B4BC booth at our K-1 Lodge and join us in a tribute ride from the top of K-1 at 2:45 p.m. Plus, DJ Benny Shreds will be playing a live set on the K-1 Patio from 2:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. for a lively afternoon of après. At 4:00 p.m. on Saturday, we’re also hosting a special film screening of Queen of the Catskills in our Snowshed Lodge. The fun is only getting started!

Shout out to our grooming team for resurfacing our slopes again early this morning. A good chunk of our trails got groomed twice, making for a much smoother surface than what things could have been following last night’s freeze. Without them, we wouldn’t have nearly as much terrain open, so hats off to the crew for doing a great job out there!

Tonight, our crew has big plans and hopes to cover around 80 runs across the mountain in fresh corduroy. If you are in for mellow cruisers, check out Easy Street, Snowshed Slope, and Pipe Dream. Panic Button, Downdraft, and Outer Limits will also be getting a groom for those looking to take on steep terrain for a fun challenge. Conditions on groomed terrain will consist of machine-groomed hardpack and frozen granular, and overall, things are riding much better than expected given the weather roller coaster we’ve been on over the past couple of weeks. 

When it gets warmer and softens up early next week, there's a good change we'll be able to open more natural terrain back up. For now, head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of options. 

Saturday is calling for clear skies with pockets of sunshine in the mix. Temps will be right around 10 degrees by the time we open, and it's expected to warm up into the teens by the afternoon. With temps as cold as they are, you already know we're firing the snowguns back on. Snowmaking is coming back online on Superstar and Mouse Trap around 8:00 a.m. and we should see some great production throughout the day. It’s already spring, but we’re still keeping winter alive as long as we can!  

We're scheduled to have 17 lifts up and turning on Saturday. It's the final weekend for Skyeship Gondola Stage I, so the next couple of days are your last chances for peak to creek laps. The Bear Mountain Quad is opening for early laps one final time at 7:30 a.m. on Saturday. Park at Bear and score a free coffee hot chocolate in the Bear Mountain Lodge from 7:00 a.m. - 9:00 a.m. Other key lifts are scheduled to open at 8:00 a.m.

In kparks, the parks at Bear (Dream Maker & The Stash) are exopectyed to reopen on Saturday with a full lineup of features. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. 

This weekend will be your last chance to take advantage of our Meet the Mountains Tours, taking place daily at 10:00 and 11:00 a.m., weather permitting. Meet your friendly tour guide in the courtyard at Snowshed Base Lodge and hit the slopes for a 75–90-minute tour of Killington Resort. 

There's so much to enjoy even this late in the season, and the party is far from over. Have fun out there! 

- Austin

name: Snow Report 2026-03-26 05:49:35
report:
Good morning, Beast family, 

Clear and sunny skies met soft spring snow yesterday and we had a blast shredding around. There’s more to be psyched about as this weekend looks to bring more sunshine. 

Today brings snow showers before 9:00 a.m., then scattered rain showers and strong winds. Temperatures will be pushing to 49 at the base and 43 at the top with just a trace of new snow at elevation.

Surface conditions are holding some hardpack still, but trails are softening up, especially where the sun shines strong like over at Bear. Skyeburst and Dream Maker had beautifully soft snow that made for a lot of fun yesterday. We could see some precipitation, but temperatures should stay well above freezing keeping surfaces on the softer trend. Head over to Bear and to Needles Eye for that more carvable snow. 

Our grooming team added Upper Wildfire to the list of trails that will be resurfaced by the time first chair rolls around. East Fall and Highline will be groomed and were holding decent surfaces. Head over to Caper or Bear Claw for a more chill ride that also got smoothed over. For the full list of groomed options, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch last week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander. Stay tuned for what's to come as they’ll be getting the guns going again soon, and this is the latest they've ever had them running! If you haven’t yet, check out our latest Insider Blog to see how our recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open again at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our South Ridge Quad coming online at 10:00 a.m. 

In our kparks, the Bear parks (Dream Maker & The Stash) are back on the menu for some sweet spring skiing & riding. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. 

Last thing: if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. 

Let’s get out there. 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-25 05:50:02
report:
Good morning, Beast fans! 

We saw a big improvement in our snow surfaces yesterday after our groomers worked some more magic and today should bring more of the same sugary snow goodness on the slopes. We added a number of trails back to our count and are monitoring (and hoping for) some potential snow coming today/tomorrow. 

For today’s weather, we’re looking at temps starting in the high 20s and creeping up to the mid 30s by the time lifts stop spinning. Predominant cloud cover will return during the day, and stronger winds will open up the day before weakening a bit through the afternoon. 

Conditions out there recently have ranged from wintry in the morning to downright springy by the afternoon. My personal run of the day yesterday was Lower East Fall, which had some really fun snow that was soft but edge-able all the way down. I didn’t need to head to Bear Mountain to find the sun yesterday, but I made a quick trip over anyway. Lower Wildfire had very soft snow in the sun, while Middle Superstar held more of its bite in the shade. It’s going to be a while before that melts out! 

Our grooming covered much of our open terrain again, with Lower Double Dipper and Downdraft getting swapped out for other steeper pitches like Panic Button and Lower Pipe Dream. Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open again at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our Canyon and South Ridge Quads coming online at 10:00 a.m.
